crime.value: Multiple murders by Jax using a knife, with accomplice luring victims to abandoned cabin
crime.quote: He saw my knife, so… he had to die.

victim.value: Multiple victims including bullies who targeted the narrator and a boy who discovered the mystery girl's knife
victim.quote: He saw my knife, so… he had to die.

closed_world.value: Torn Isaac High School and the abandoned cabin by the river where murders take place
closed_world.quote: We went to the same elementary and high school. Torn Isaac High School.

culprit_and_motive.value: Jax commits murders to protect himself and the narrator from bullies and witnesses; motivated by his warped philosophy about murder
culprit_and_motive.quote: What is murder? Can it really be defined? Murder in self-defense isn't considered murder, but accidental murder is?

investigator.value: The unnamed narrator who serves as Jax's accomplice and observes the crimes

suspect_architecture.value: Multiple killers across interconnected storylines: Jax (primary murderer), unnamed narrator (accomplice), mystery girl with knife, Bailey's mother coordinating military operatives

red_herring.value: Bailey appears to be set up as bait or false target while her mother orchestrates the real operation using military personnel
red_herring.quote: You're free today, Bailey. Enjoy your time.

social_world.value: School hierarchy enabling bullying and murder, military/paramilitary organization with family connections, government death penalty system questioned
social_world.quote: He believed that the government murdered their rivals and criminals through the death sentence

alibi.value: Jax claims no connection to missing bullies while narrator finds the bodies, creating false deniability
alibi.quote: Jax would claim that he had no connection to them, but I found the bodies.

reveal_mechanic.value: Red light on explosive device reveals the trap, physical evidence of C-4 demonstrates the murder method

investigator_wound.value: Narrator trapped as unwilling accomplice, years of regret and sorrow from being controlled and forced to lure victims
investigator_wound.quote: Years and years of blindly following his every order racked up regrets and sorrows.
